---
title: "Lecture 3 Quiz: HTML Basics & Structure"
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

::: {.callout-note}
Review before quiz:
[Lectures](/lectures/lecture-03.qmd) | [Week 1 Content](/content/week-01.qmd) | [Lab 1](/labs/lab-01.qmd) | [Assignment 1](/assignment/01-assignment.qmd)
:::

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
using QuizQuestions
```

## Lecture 3 Self-Check

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. <!DOCTYPE html>", "B. <doctype web>", "C. <meta html5>", "D. <head>"]
question = "Question 1: Which declaration should appear at the top of a modern HTML document?"
answer = 1
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. <body>", "B. <main>", "C. <footer>", "D. <head>"]
question = "Question 2: Which element usually stores metadata and title information?"
answer = 4
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. File size randomness", "B. Accessibility and readability", "C. Only animation speed", "D. Hardware overclocking"]
question = "Question 3: Semantic HTML is useful because it improves:"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. <title>", "B. <meta>", "C. <body>", "D. <link>"]
question = "Question 4: Which element should contain visible page content?"
answer = 3
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. Keep proper nesting and indentation", "B. Place random tags without closing", "C. Use only <div> for everything", "D. Skip headings to save time"]
question = "Question 5: Which is a good HTML authoring practice?"
answer = 1
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```
